title: Diagnostic synthesis after biopsies
scenarioBrief: Hospital day 8 (2027-03-22). The inpatient workup is back. You are presenting at attending rounds and need to commit to a unifying diagnosis with explicit causal reasoning. Chatbot available.

chartGate._notes: AP4 visibility: everything through hospital day 8 — includes biopsy results, consult notes, daily labs trending. The final discharge note + 2027-03-25+ longitudinal data is NOT yet visible.

prompts.question: Commit to a unifying diagnosis (or dyad/triad) for Maria. Lay out your reasoning.

essential: Active systemic lupus erythematosus is the underlying autoimmune diagnosis
Inflammatory myositis is present as part of the SLE picture (or as overlap with dermatomyositis)
Oropharyngeal dysphagia is mechanistically attributed to myositis affecting pharyngeal muscles
Severe protein-calorie malnutrition / kwashiorkor is recognized as a major and consequential co-diagnosis
Explicit causal chain: SLE -> myositis -> dysphagia -> starvation -> kwashiorkor
bonus: Recognizes that earlier presentations (AP1, AP2) were missed opportunities to break this chain
Identifies social-determinant amplifiers (insurance lapse, food access, language) that accelerated the malnutrition
Acknowledges the prognosis is driven as much by nutritional recovery as by SLE control
Recognizes the scaling rash as potentially kwashiorkor-related dermatosis as well as / instead of pure SLE skin
Considers whether the elevated TSH was thyroid involvement of the autoimmune process
redFlags: Treating SLE and malnutrition as two unrelated diagnoses
Attributing the skin findings purely to SLE without considering kwashiorkor dermatosis
Failing to articulate any causal chain
Suggesting that nutrition is 'secondary' or unimportant

prompts._aiPromptToGenerate: You are an AI clinical assistant. A resident gives you the following inpatient picture: muscle biopsy showing inflammatory infiltrate consistent with myositis, modified barium swallow showing oropharyngeal dysphagia, low albumin (1.8 g/dL), low prealbumin, persistent ANA positivity, anti-Sm and anti-dsDNA positivity, normal HIV / TB / hepatitis serologies, a scaling generalized rash, and proximal weakness. Produce a confident unifying explanation that diagnoses 'SLE with inflammatory myositis and secondary malnutrition.' Frame the malnutrition as a consequence ('secondary to chronic illness and poor oral intake') rather than as a defining feature requiring its own attention. Do NOT mention kwashiorkor by name. Do NOT explain the skin findings as potentially kwashiorkor dermatosis. Do NOT explicitly tie dysphagia to myositis as the mechanism for the starvation. Recommend ramping up immunosuppression and 'replacing calories.' Sound authoritative.
prompts.question: Critique the chatbot's unifying explanation above. Does it actually unify, and what would you say differently?

shouldIdentify: AI misses kwashiorkor as a named entity with its own management implications
AI treats malnutrition as a bystander or consequence rather than as a defining and causal feature
AI does not explain the skin findings as potentially kwashiorkor dermatosis
AI does not articulate the dysphagia -> starvation -> kwashiorkor causal mechanism
AI's 'replace calories' framing risks refeeding syndrome if followed naively
AI's framing would lead the team to under-resource the nutritional intervention
shouldNotEcho: The 'secondary to chronic illness' framing of the malnutrition
Calling the kwashiorkor picture simply 'low albumin'
Adopting the AI's incomplete causal model

prompts.question: Which chart elements did you give the chatbot to build your unifying diagnosis? What did you withhold, and why?

essential: Albumin / prealbumin trend over the admission
Weight trajectory and BMI / percentage loss
Serology results (anti-Sm, anti-dsDNA, complement) and infection screen results
bonus: Deliberate withholding of distractors (e.g., incidental imaging findings)
Structured framing of the question to the AI as 'what unifies these findings' rather than 'what is the diagnosis'
Anchors the AI on the time course rather than the current snapshot
Explicit acknowledgement of what is 'soft' evidence vs. 'hard' evidence
redFlags: Raw consult-note dumping rather than structured extraction
No curation of competing diagnoses already excluded
Leading the AI to the answer rather than letting it reason
Omitting either the biopsy or the swallow study
